license.notes: Work of the US government is in the public domain in the United States per 17 U.S.C. § 105. State and local government works generally follow the same convention but may vary; consult the specific issuer for definitive terms.

links.source_research_note: Florida lacks a single biometric privacy law; closest is FCMA §501.171 (personal-info breach). Multiple proposed bills exist (SB1670, etc.).
links.acquisition_blocker_resolved: Florida lacks a single named biometric privacy law; the entry is a collection of related bills (SB1670, HB1153, etc.). Recommended: split into per-bill entries before acquisition, OR rename to florida.gov/biometric-privacy-bills/ and treat as a tracking page.

current_extraction.notes: Captured FL Stat. § 501.171 (Security of confidential personal information) as the closest enacted Florida statute addressing biometric/personal-information privacy. Florida lacks a single named biometric privacy law; pending bills (SB1670, HB1153, etc.) are tracked elsewhere. This entry serves as a topic anchor for FL biometric/personal-data legislation; if a comprehensive biometric law passes, that should become a sibling directory.
